game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept:
The five crew members of the Tulpar are stranded in the empty reaches of space, shrouded in perpetual sunset. God is not watching.

Mouthwashing is a first-person horror game following the dying crew of a shipwrecked space freighter.


Who could have known what good ol’ Captain Curly was capable of? Guess he thought his crew dying alongside him was only right. But some men can’t even kill themselves properly. Maimed, limbless and unable to speak, but alive, Curly is now at the mercy of the crew he has doomed to a slow death.

Descend into Madness

Follow the lives of the crew as they weather starvation, isolation, and each other. People were never your strong suit anyway.

Zero Chance of Rescue

The ship will run out of power within six months. Food rations long before that.

Immersive Storytelling

Pay attention to your surroundings.

Psychological Scares

Your eyes are lying.

Unforgiving Narrative

Hope to die, or for goodness sake, pray that everyone else did.
game_info.description: You are Jimmy, the co-pilot of the Tulpar freighter, drifting through space after a catastrophic crash. Survive for as long as possible while managing dwindling food rations and maintaining the ship's critical systems. Keep your sanity intact as isolation and guilt weigh on your mind. The ship's AI will eventually fail, and rescue is impossible. How long can you last before the darkness consumes you?
game_info.controls:
Arrow Keys: Move Jimmy through the ship
Space: Interact with objects (food storage, ship systems, crew quarters)
Z: Check status (health, sanity, days survived)
Enter: Start game
Escape: Pause/Unpause
R: Restart (from game over screen)

TEST_1.test_description: Basic movement and interaction test. Validates that the player can navigate the ship and interact with objects. Tests collision detection and basic game mechanics.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Move through all accessible rooms, interact with food storage and ship systems periodically. Avoid staying idle to test movement responsiveness and game state updates.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: Player successfully moves through rooms, interactions register properly, and game state updates correctly. No errors or collision issues occur during navigation.

TEST_2.test_description: Survival optimization test. Tests the optimal strategy to maximize survival time by balancing resource management and system maintenance.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Prioritize food consumption when hunger is high, maintain ship systems before they fail, and balance sanity management by visiting different areas. Use efficient pathing to conserve energy.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: Player survives for an extended period (30+ days) by effectively managing resources. Game eventually ends in GAME_OVER_LOSE when resources are depleted, validating win/lose conditions.

TEST_3.test_description: Sanity degradation test. Validates that the psychological horror mechanics work correctly and that sanity affects gameplay.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Deliberately avoid sanity-restoring actions and let sanity degrade. Test visual distortions and hallucination effects as sanity decreases.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: Sanity decreases over time, visual effects intensify, and eventually leads to game over if sanity reaches zero. Validates the psychological horror mechanics.
